steps.title: Convert "158% greater" to a growth multiplier
steps.content: If the mass is 158% greater than the previous period, the new mass equals the old mass plus 158% of the old mass: $M_{\text{new}} = M_{\text{old}} + 1.58 \cdot M_{\text{old}} = 2.58 \cdot M_{\text{old}}$. The growth factor per 5-week period is $2.58$.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content: Starting from initial mass $M_0$, after $t$ weeks the number of 5-week periods elapsed is $\frac{t}{5}$. The model is: $M = M_0 \cdot (2.58)^{t/5}$. The exponent must be $\frac{t}{5}$, not $\frac{5}{t}$, so that M grows as t increases.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $M = 538.92(1.58)^{5/t}$ — Uses multiplier 1.58 (not 2.58) and wrong exponent $\frac{5}{t}$. Eliminated.
  • B: $M = 457.66(2.58)^{5/t}$ — Correct multiplier but wrong exponent direction $\frac{5}{t}$; also wrong initial mass. Eliminated.
  • C: $M = 159.14(1.58)^{t/5}$ — Uses multiplier 1.58 instead of 2.58. Eliminated.
  • D: $M = 36.55(2.58)^{t/5}$ — Multiplier 2.58 and exponent $\frac{t}{5}$ are both correct, and $M_0 \approx 36.55$ matches our calculation. That matches choice D — D is correct.
